title: Grain shippers want regulators to press railroads on service issues
text: U.S. grain shippers are asking federal regulators to press the Class I railroads to relieve “significant” service disruptions throughout the national rail network. In a letter to the Surface Transportation Board, the National Grain and Feed Association (NGFA) said at least one of the group's members had spent $3 million on secondary freight because the member didn't have the animal feed it would otherwise receive from a rail shipment.

title: Union Pacific Announces Largest-Ever Disaster Relief Donation to Support Ukrainian Humanitarian Relief
text: OMAHA, Neb. , March 23, 2022 /PRNewswire/ --Union Pacific is making its largest-ever corporate disaster relief donation of $500,000 to support humanitarian relief in Ukraine.

title: Union Pacific (UNP) Upgraded to Buy: Here's What You Should Know
text: Union Pacific (UNP) has been upgraded to a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), reflecting growing optimism about the company's earnings prospects. This might drive the stock higher in the near term.

title: What To Expect From Union Pacific In 2022
text: Union Pacific is trading at an all-time high despite a market correction. The company is set to benefit from rebounding automotive production and strong exports in coal, agriculture, and related.

title: Union Pacific: A Dividend Growth Machine
text: Shareholders of Union Pacific Corporation ( UNP , Financial) have seen their dividend payments compound at a rate of nearly 15% over the last decade. This is one of the highest growth rates among companies in the transportation industry.

title: Union Pacific (UNP) Partners With Wabtec to Reduce Emissions
text: Union Pacific's (UNP) partnership with Wabtec is aimed at helping the former increase the percentage of low-carbon fuel consumed to 10% of its total diesel consumption by 2025 and 20% by 2030.

title: Union Pacific to test higher biodiesel blends on Wabtec locomotives
text: Union Pacific will be testing higher biodiesel blends for the locomotives it acquired from Wabtec as part of UP's wider efforts to increase the consumption of low-carbon fuels over the next eight years. Starting in the second quarter, UP (NYSE: UP) will be testing B20 biodiesel and R55 renewable diesel on Wabtec FDL engines operating in California, with plans to up the percentage of biofuels used as testing continues.
